spec.reasoning: The task involves cross-sheet lookup/sum using SUMPRODUCT. I rename sheets (book1→summary, book2→data) and the key column (Place→Location) to break hardcoded references. Both sheets' key column get the same rename to preserve alignment. I add a distractor column 'Region' to the data sheet after the last column, which is plausibly similar to 'Location' but clearly different. I avoid blank_row_insert because the risk flags indicate 'task_references_specific_cell_addresses' - the instruction explicitly references book1!A3 and book2!H:J, and inserting a blank row would shift data and break these references. The distractor column is added after the last column so it doesn't shift H:J positions. The 'Place' column name does not appear as a common English word in the instruction body (it's used as a column header reference), so renaming it is safe.
